title: Decision Ledger
description: Append-only ADR ledger for the project. Entries are never deleted or renumbered; a reversed decision is superseded by a new entry. Designs and briefs cite ADR IDs instead of restating decisions.

updated.description: ISO 8601 date of the last edit to this ledger

id.description: Decision ID, sequential per project, never reused

title.description: The decision in a phrase (e.g. 'No default timeouts anywhere in the engine')

status.description: proposed = recommendation recorded, awaiting the call; decided = in force; superseded = replaced (superseded_by names the successor)

scope.description: Where this binds: 'project' for cross-cutting decisions, otherwise a cluster name

date.description: ISO 8601 date of the status-setting event (the decision date once decided); empty string while proposed and undated

decided_by.description: Who made the call; empty string while proposed

context.description: Why this came up: the problem, the incident, the fork in the road. Enough that a reader who wasn't there understands the pressure.

decision.description: The call itself, with the rejected alternative named. If there is no rejected alternative it was not a decision.

quote.description: Verbatim words of the decider when they carry the intent; empty string when none. Quotes survive summarisation; paraphrase does not.

consequences.description: What follows: obligations created, doors closed, work implied

superseded_by.description: The ADR ID that replaced this one; empty string while the decision stands
